# Source Card: Self-reported Race, Serum Creatinine, Cystatin C, and GFR in Children and Young Adults With Pediatric Kidney Diseases (CKiD Study)

## Summary
This peer-reviewed CKiD cohort study (AJKD 2021, PMID 34974031) characterizes how self-reported race relates to measured GFR after accounting for serum creatinine or cystatin C in children and young adults with CKD, and validates the race-independent "U25" (under age 25) eGFR equations. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies method-anchored evidence that the U25 creatinine-only equation underestimates mGFR in self-reported Black participants, the cystatin C-only equation has no significant race bias, and the average of the two U25 equations is unbiased with the best accuracy metrics. It does NOT define the U25 equation coefficients/thresholds themselves.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) U25 creatinine-only eGFR underestimated mGFR by -3.37 mL/min/1.73 m2 in Black participants; cystatin C-only had no race bias — locator: Results, U25 performance section — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f2bea23b384cbaff82d5c7ffbbb7aad39bad2a627024423966c5100b3785496e]"
- (ev_002) Self-reported Black race associated with 12.8% higher mGFR adjusting for SCr — locator: Results, creatinine section — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5619fd582fb53c87a2ab5ceeaf34c932d715dbbeb68376e4d472691739ab019a]"
- (ev_003) Self-reported Black race associated with 3.5% lower mGFR adjusting for CysC (attenuates with age) — locator: Results, cystatin C section — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c3a2879eb74e3c2e98ddb23ceaffd5bc316c286acfec48c12271b6bc07d28f95]"
- (ev_004) Average of U25 SCr and CysC equations is unbiased with best 30%/10% accuracy and lowest RMSE — locator: Results, U25 performance section — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:372ea3f8becf423628ea42a095b5269ec7f308f75db905374bc62642969aa569]"
- (ev_005) Assay/method spec: iohexol mGFR, Haycock BSA standardization; IFCC-calibrated SCr (mg/dL) and CysC (mg/L) — locator: Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:67112fdc902473d21cd81e42d3d5278a375ce0350d03b29c5d9c93b724d3b65a]"
- (ev_006) Cohort: 190 Black (473 visits), 675 non-Black (1,897 visits), median age 9; median SCr 1.2 mg/dL both groups — locator: Results opening / Table 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ebbbc99990d9228e078e036aa851bca2fba5b56bcb129a902fae97bd9e97ad3c]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children and young adults <25 with a CKD diagnosis (CKiD), median age 9 years; age strata <6, 6–12, 12–18, >=18 years; both sexes (~61–66% male). Race is self- or parental-reported (binary Black / non-Black), not genetic ancestry.
- Assay/method dependence: measured GFR = plasma iohexol clearance, BSA-standardized by the Haycock formula. SCr and CysC are IFCC-calibrated central-lab measurements (SCr in mg/dL, CysC in mg/L); numeric biomarker values are method-dependent. The U25 equations are creatinine- and/or cystatin-C-based, race-independent.
- Lifecycle: findings published 2021-12 (AJKD vol 80(2):174-185); no guideline version, no retirement/supersession stated in this source. This paper validates but does not author the U25 equations.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- None. This source defines no diagnostic cutoff, red-flag, or referral trigger. It is evidence on GFR-estimation biomarker behavior and equation accuracy by race — an input to the CDS eGFR-estimation logic, not an executable rule, diagnosis, or dosing directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Cohort-count discrepancy WITHIN the source: the Results body and Table 1 state 190 Black / 675 non-Black and 473 / 1,897 person-visits, while the structured Abstract states 191 Black / 674 non-Black and 474 / 1,896 person-visits. Preserved as a conflict; body/Table figures used above (ev_006). Not collapsed or averaged.
- Unit inconsistency WITHIN the source: the Results opening paragraph prints median cystatin C as "1.51 vs. 1.73 mg/dL", whereas Methods specify cystatin C in mg/L. The Methods unit (mg/L) is the correct/canonical one; the mg/dL in the Table-1 sentence appears to be a typographical error. Preserved verbatim in ev_006 with the discrepancy flagged.
- GAP — U25 equation coefficients/thresholds NOT locatable in this source: the actual U25 creatinine and cystatin C equation forms (coefficients, k values, exponents) are not printed in this paper; it validates equations developed elsewhere (referenced as prior CKiD/U25 work). Any numeric equation constant must be sourced from the U25 derivation paper, not from src_20260718_rfkid001_01.
- Generalizability: all participants have a CKD diagnosis; results may not extend to children without kidney disease. The <6-year subgroup is small (n=46 person-visits) and several race differences there were not statistically significant. Lean body mass was estimated, not measured; no genetic-ancestry data.
- No conflict recorded against other cited sources at this time (conflicts_with empty); the within-source discrepancies above are the only conflicts noted.
